name: Burling Bank
description_short: Independent Chicago-based community bank founded in 1989, offering FDIC-insured deposit products, personal banking, wealth management, and specialized business lending solutions.
description_long:
Burling Bank is an independent community bank established in 1989 and headquartered in Chicago. The bank is named after Edward J. Burling, a 19th-century Chicago architect who pioneered the city's reconstruction after the Great Chicago Fire of 1871. This historical connection informs the bank's philosophy of community-centric banking with a focus on personal relationships and local economic development in the Chicagoland area.

Burling Bank offers a comprehensive range of banking services across personal and commercial segments. Personal offerings include personal banking online, private banking with personalized service, and private wealth management. For businesses, the bank provides small business banking, corporate banking, commercial lending, and specialized innovation banking for startups and entrepreneurs. All deposit products are FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. Additional services include Burling Wealth Management (investment solutions), Burling Asset Management (institutional cash management), and specialized nonprofit banking solutions.

The bank distinguishes itself through emphasis on personal connection, transparency, and innovation tailored to Chicago-area clients. They actively promote fraud prevention education and maintain surcharge-free ATM access. Burling explicitly positions itself as an alternative to larger national banks by offering community impact focus and industry-specific expertise, particularly in innovation banking for startups and support for local nonprofits and entrepreneurs.

As a regional community bank, Burling Bank's reach is geographically limited to the Chicagoland area, which may not suit customers seeking national branch networks. While the website demonstrates strong fraud prevention and security messaging, specific details about product rates, fees, account minimums, and competitive terms are not disclosed on the publicly available pages reviewed.
services: Private banking with personalized service and comprehensive solutions
Asset management for corporations and institutions
pros: FDIC-insured deposit products backed by full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Specialized innovation banking services designed specifically for startups and innovation economy stakeholders
Dedicated nonprofit banking solutions with checking, lending, liquidity, and asset management tools
Surcharge-free ATM locator and access network for customers
Community-focused approach with emphasis on Chicago area economic development and local entrepreneurship
Private banking and wealth management services offering personalized comprehensive solutions
Strong fraud prevention education resources and transparency around what the bank will never request
cons: Geographic limitation to Chicagoland area only—not accessible to customers outside Chicago metropolitan region
Wealth management and asset management services are not FDIC-insured and may lose value
Limited public transparency on website regarding specific account fees, interest rates, minimum balances, or competitive pricing
No information provided about mobile banking capabilities or digital service features
As a smaller regional bank, may lack the extensive branch network and service infrastructure of national banks
best_for: Chicago-area small business owners seeking personalized lending and banking relationships
Local startup founders and innovation economy participants needing specialized banking services
High-net-worth individuals in Chicagoland seeking private banking and wealth management
Chicago nonprofits and community organizations requiring philanthropic-focused banking solutions
diagnosis: Burling Bank is best for Chicago-area residents and businesses seeking a relationship-driven community bank alternative to national institutions, with particular strength in small business and innovation banking. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to Chicagoland—this bank is not an option for customers outside the Chicago metropolitan area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
